findings.title: User Behavior Tracking Pipeline Dependency
findings.description: The implementation of the ML recommendation engine (PROF-102) is dependent on the successful deployment of the user behavior tracking pipeline (PROF-101). Without the data ingestion pipeline capturing user interactions, the recommendation engine will lack the necessary training data.
assumptions: The user behavior tracking pipeline will be built and operational before the recommendation engine is developed.

evidence.excerpt: Implement a data ingestion pipeline that captures browsing history, click patterns, scroll depth, and time-on-page metrics for each authenticated user.
Build a recommendation engine that uses accumulated user profile data.

recommendations: Prioritize the development of the user behavior tracking pipeline.

findings.title: GDPR Compliance Risk
findings.description: The PersonaLens profiling system requires a full GDPR compliance audit before launch. This is a critical blocker for the EU market launch, as legal concerns regarding data processing, consent mechanisms, and data retention policies have been flagged.
assumptions: The GDPR compliance audit will be completed before any data processing begins.

evidence.excerpt: Legal team has flagged that the PersonaLens profiling system requires a full GDPR compliance audit before launch.

recommendations: Conduct the GDPR compliance audit as a priority before proceeding with other developments.

findings.title: Phased Delivery Plan Proposal
findings.description: Propose a phased delivery plan for the PersonaLens project. MVP should include the user behavior tracking pipeline (PROF-101) and GDPR compliance audit (PROF-106). V1 can include the ML recommendation engine (PROF-102) and user preference dashboard (PROF-103). V2 can include cross-device tracking (PROF-104) and data export for advertisers (PROF-105).
assumptions: The team can deliver each phase within the estimated timelines.

evidence.excerpt: Implement a data ingestion pipeline that captures browsing history.

recommendations: Implement the phased delivery plan as outlined.

findings.title: Cross-Device Tracking Dependency
findings.description: The implementation of cross-device tracking (PROF-104) is dependent on the successful creation of a cross-device identity graph. This graph is essential for linking user activity across devices and is a prerequisite for a unified user profile.
assumptions: The cross-device identity graph will be developed concurrently with the user behavior tracking pipeline.

evidence.excerpt: Requires a cross-device identity graph stored in our profile database.

recommendations: Ensure the development of the cross-device identity graph is prioritized.

findings.title: Data Export Compliance Risk
findings.description: The data export system for advertisers (PROF-105) poses a risk due to legal concerns regarding anonymization. The legal team has flagged that individual re-identification may be possible with enough cohort intersections, which could lead to compliance issues.
assumptions: The anonymization pipeline will be validated before the data export system is implemented.

evidence.excerpt: However, the anonymization pipeline needs validation — legal has flagged that with enough cohort intersections, individual re-identification may be possible.

recommendations: Validate the anonymization pipeline before proceeding with the data export system.

summary: The analysis identifies critical dependencies and risks associated with the PersonaLens User Profiling project. Key dependencies include the user behavior tracking pipeline and GDPR compliance audit, both of which are essential for the project's success. A phased delivery plan is recommended to ensure timely execution while addressing compliance and legal concerns.
